app.title: 🎛️ ACE-Step V1.5 Playground💡
app.subtitle: Pushing the Boundaries of Open-Source Music Generation

dataset.title: 📊 Dataset Explorer
dataset.search_value_placeholder: Enter keys or index (leave empty for random)
dataset.search_value_info: Keys: exact match, Index: 0 to dataset size-1.
dataset.use_src_info: Check to use the source audio from dataset.

service.title: 🔧 Service Configuration
service.checkpoint_info: Select a trained model checkpoint file (full path or filename).
service.model_path_info: Select the model configuration directory (auto-scanned from checkpoints).
service.lm_model_path_info: Select the 5Hz LM model checkpoint (auto-scanned from checkpoints).
service.backend_info: Select backend for 5Hz LM: vllm (faster) or pt (PyTorch, more compatible).
service.init_llm_info: Check to initialize 5Hz LM during service initialization.
service.lm_unavailable_vram: ⚠️ LM not available for this GPU tier (VRAM too low)
service.flash_attention_info_enabled: Enable flash attention for faster inference (requires flash_attn package).
service.flash_attention_info_disabled: Flash attention not available (flash_attn package not installed).
service.offload_cpu_info: Offload models to CPU when not in use to save GPU memory.
service.offload_dit_cpu_info: Offload DiT to CPU (needs Offload to CPU).
service.compile_model_info: Use torch.compile to optimize model (required for quantization).
service.quantization_info: Enable INT8 weight-only quantization to reduce VRAM usage (requires Compile Model).
service.mlx_dit_info_enabled: Use native MLX for DiT diffusion on Apple Silicon (faster than MPS).
service.mlx_dit_info_disabled: MLX not available (requires macOS + Apple Silicon + mlx package).
service.tier_info: Manually select GPU tier to adjust optimization defaults (offload, quantization, backend, etc.).

generation.task_type_info: Select the task type for generation.
generation.instruction_info: Instruction is automatically generated based on task type.
generation.track_name_info: Select track name for lego/extract tasks.
generation.track_classes_info: Select multiple track classes for complete task.
generation.lm_codes_info: Paste LM codes hints for text2music generation.
generation.mode_info: Select a generation mode to get started.
generation.mode_info_simple: Describe your music in natural language. AI will generate caption, lyrics and metadata for you.
generation.mode_info_custom: Full control over caption, lyrics and all parameters.
generation.mode_info_remix: Upload source audio to create a remix version with your caption and lyrics.
generation.mode_info_repaint: Upload source audio and repaint a specific time range.
generation.mode_info_extract: Extract a specific track (vocals, drums, etc.) from source audio.
generation.mode_info_lego: Reassemble tracks: replace a specific track in the source audio.
generation.mode_info_complete: Complete missing tracks in the source audio.
generation.simple_query_placeholder: Describe the music you want to create, e.g., 'a soft Bengali love song for a quiet evening'. Leave empty for a random sample.
generation.simple_query_info: Enter a natural language description of the music you want to generate.
generation.simple_vocal_language_info: Select preferred language(s) for lyrics. Use 'unknown' for any language.
generation.caption_placeholder: A peaceful acoustic guitar melody with soft vocals.
generation.caption_info: Describe the style, genre, instruments, and mood.
generation.lyrics_placeholder: [Verse 1]\nUnder the starry night\nI feel so alive.
generation.guidance_scale_label: DiT Guidance Scale (Only support for base model)
generation.guidance_scale_info: Higher values follow text more closely.
generation.shift_info: Timestep shift factor for base models (range 1.0~5.0, default 3.0). Not effective for turbo models.
generation.infer_method_info: Diffusion inference method. ODE (Euler) is faster, SDE (stochastic) may produce different results.
generation.custom_timesteps_info: Comma-separated values from 1.0 to 0.0 (e.g., '0.97,0.76,0.615,0.5,0.395,0.28,0.18,0.085,0'). Overrides inference steps and shift.
generation.lm_negative_prompt_placeholder: Enter negative prompt for CFG (default: NO USER INPUT)
generation.lm_negative_prompt_info: Negative prompt (use when LM CFG Scale > 1.0).
generation.cot_metas_info: Use LM to generate CoT metadata (uncheck to skip LM CoT generation).
generation.constrained_debug_info: Enable debug logging for constrained decoding (check to see detailed logs).
generation.auto_score_info: Automatically calculate quality scores for all generated audios.
generation.auto_lrc_info: Automatically generate LRC lyrics timestamps for all generated audios.
generation.lm_batch_chunk_info: Max items per LM batch chunk (default: 8, limited by GPU memory).
generation.codes_strength_info: Control how many denoising steps use LM-generated codes.
generation.cover_strength_info: Control how many denoising steps use cover mode.
generation.remix_strength_info: Control how closely the remix follows the source audio (higher = closer to original).
generation.cover_noise_strength_info: Controls melody retention in Remix mode. Recommended: use the SFT model with a value of 0.1–0.25. A small increase restores the melody, but style transfer may require additional prompt tuning. (0 = pure noise/no cover, 1 = closest to original audio).
generation.enable_normalization_info: Normalize audio volume to target peak level to prevent clipping or ensure consistent loudness.
generation.normalization_db_info: Target peak level in decibels. -1.0 dB is standard safe peak. -0.1 dB is max.
generation.fade_in_duration_info: Apply a linear fade in to the beginning of the audio. 0 = no fade in. Range: 0–10 seconds.
generation.fade_out_duration_info: Apply a linear fade out to the end of the audio. 0 = no fade out. Range: 0–10 seconds.
generation.latent_shift_info: Shift applied to DiT latents before VAE decode. Default 0 (no shift). Negative values (e.g. -0.04) can reduce clipping.
generation.latent_rescale_info: Rescale factor for DiT latents before VAE decode. Default 1.0 (no rescale). Values < 1.0 (e.g. 0.91) can reduce clipping.
generation.score_sensitivity_info: Lower = more sensitive (default: 1.0). Adjusts how PMI maps to [0,1].
generation.lora_path_info: Path to trained LoRA adapter directory
generation.parallel_thinking_info: Process batch samples in parallel for faster generation.
generation.caption_rewrite_info: Use LM to rewrite caption before generation.

messages.simple_examples_not_found: ⚠️ Simple mode examples directory not found.
messages.simple_examples_empty: ⚠️ No example files found in simple mode examples.
messages.skipping_metas_cot: ⚡ Skipping Phase 1 metas COT (sample already formatted)
messages.invalid_timesteps_format: ⚠️ Invalid timesteps format. Using default schedule.

training.scan_info: Scan for audio files (wav, mp3, flac, ogg, opus).
training.step2_instruction:
Click the button below to use AI to automatically generate metadata for all audio files:
- **Caption**: Music style, genre, mood description
- **BPM**: Beats per minute
- **Key**: Music key (e.g. C Major, Am)
- **Time Signature**: 4/4, 3/4 etc.
training.step5_intro:
**Preprocessing converts your dataset into pre-computed tensors for fast training.**

You can:
- Use the dataset from steps 1-4 above, **OR**
- Load an existing dataset JSON file (if you have one saved)
training.step5_details:
This step will:
- Encode audio to VAE latents
- Encode captions and lyrics to text embeddings
- Run condition encoders
- Save all tensors to `.pt` files

⚠️ **This requires loading models and may take a few minutes.**
training.train_tensor_selection_desc:
Select the directory containing preprocessed tensor files (`.pt` files).
These are created using the 'Preprocess' button in the 'Dataset Builder' tab.
training.all_instrumental_info: Check if all tracks are instrumental (no vocals).
training.custom_tag_info: Unique tag to activate this LoRA style.
training.tag_position_info: Where to place the custom tag in the caption.
training.genre_ratio_info: 0%=All Caption, 100%=All Genre. Single sample override takes precedence.
training.skip_metas_info: Skip BPM/Key/TimeSig generation. Captions and genres are still generated by LM.
training.only_unlabeled_info: Only label samples with no caption (for continuing failed labeling).
training.select_sample_info: Select sample to preview and edit.
training.caption: Caption
training.prompt_override_info: Override global ratio for this sample.
training.load_existing_info: Path to previously saved dataset JSON file.
training.tensor_output_info: Directory to save preprocessed tensor files.
training.output_dir_info: Directory to save trained LoRA weights.
training.lokr_no_checkpoints_use_latest: ℹ No checkpoints found; export will use latest available weights
training.lokr_no_exportable_checkpoints: ℹ No exportable epoch checkpoints found
training.lokr_no_weights_selected_epoch: ❌ No LoKr weights found for selected epoch: {epoch}
training.lokr_no_weights_latest_checkpoint: ❌ No LoKr weights found in latest checkpoint: {checkpoint}
training.lokr_no_trained_weights_found: ❌ No trained LoKr weights found in {path}
training.lokr_tensor_selection_desc:
Select the directory containing preprocessed tensor files (`.pt` files).
These are created using the 'Preprocess' button in the 'Dataset Builder' tab.
training.lokr_linear_alpha_info: Scaling factor for LoKr (usually similar to dim).
training.lokr_decompose_both_info: When enabled, decomposes both left and right matrices.
training.lokr_use_scalar_info: Enable scalar gating for LoKr weights.
training.lokr_weight_decompose_info: Enable weight decomposition for more stable LoKr training.
training.lokr_learning_rate_info: LoKr commonly uses a higher LR than LoRA. Tune per dataset.
training.lokr_output_dir_info: Directory to save trained LoKr weights.
training.lokr_checkpoint_epoch_info: Select a specific epoch checkpoint to export, or keep Latest (auto).

help.getting_started:
## Getting Started

1. **Select a model** in the Settings accordion (e.g. `acestep-v15-turbo`)
2. **Choose a 5Hz LM** if you want Thinking mode (recommended)
3. Click **Initialize Service** and wait for the green status
4. Pick a **Generation Mode** (start with Simple)
5. Describe your music and click **Generate Music**

> **Tip:** Turbo models are fastest. Enable "Think" for smarter generation.
help.service_config:
## Service Configuration

### Quick Setup
1. Select **Main Model Path** (turbo recommended)
2. Select **5Hz LM Model Path** (auto-filtered by GPU)
3. Choose **Backend**: `vllm` (fast, NVIDIA ≥8GB) or `pt` (universal)
4. Check **Initialize 5Hz LM** for Thinking mode
5. Click **Initialize Service**

### Performance Tips
- **Flash Attention**: Faster inference (needs flash_attn)
- **CPU Offload**: Auto-enabled on GPUs <20GB
- **INT8 Quantization**: Reduces VRAM, auto-enabled <20GB
- **Compile**: Required for quantization, enabled by default

### LoRA
- Set LoRA path → Load → Enable "Use LoRA"
- ⚠️ Cannot use LoRA with INT8 quantization
help.generation_simple:
## Simple Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Quick music creation with minimal effort.

### Steps
1. Select **Simple** in Generation Mode
2. Type a description, e.g. *"upbeat pop song with catchy guitar riff"*
3. (Optional) Check **Instrumental** for no vocals
4. (Optional) Select **Vocal Language**
5. Click **Create Sample** — AI generates caption, lyrics, metadata
6. Review and edit the generated content if needed
7. Click **Generate Music**

### Tips
- Click 🎲 for random inspiration
- The more specific your description, the better the result
- Leave BPM/Key empty to let AI decide
help.generation_custom:
## Custom Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Full creative control over every parameter.

### Steps
1. Select **Custom** in Generation Mode
2. Write a detailed **Caption** describing style, genre, instruments, mood
3. Write **Lyrics** with structure tags: `[Verse]`, `[Chorus]`, `[Bridge]`
4. (Optional) Upload **Reference Audio** for style guidance
5. Set **BPM**, **Key**, **Duration** or leave empty for auto
6. Click **Format** to enhance with LM (optional)
7. Click **Generate Music**

### Caption Tips
- Be specific: *"dreamy shoegaze with reverb-heavy guitars and whispered vocals"*
- Include: genre, instruments, mood, tempo feel, vocal style
help.generation_remix:
## Remix Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Creating cover versions or style transfers.

### Steps
1. Select **Remix** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio** (the song to remix)
3. Write a **Caption** describing the target style
4. (Optional) Modify **Lyrics**
5. Adjust **Remix Strength** (0.0–1.0):
   - Higher = closer to original structure
   - Lower = more creative freedom
6. Click **Generate Music**

### Tips
- Start with strength 0.5 and adjust
- Use SFT model with Cover Strength 0.1–0.25 for melody retention
help.generation_repaint:
## Repaint Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Fixing or regenerating specific sections of generated music while keeping the rest intact.

### Steps
1. Select **Repaint** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Set **Repainting Start** (seconds) and **End** (-1 for end of file)
4. Write a **Caption** for the repainted section
5. Choose a **Repaint Mode** and adjust **Repaint Strength** if needed
6. Click **Generate Music**

### Repaint Mode
Controls how the model treats non-repainted regions:

- **Conservative** (Strength = 0): Maximum source preservation. Source latents are injected at every diffusion step with long boundary crossfades. Best for seamless blending.
- **Balanced** (default): Source latents are injected for a fraction of steps controlled by the Repaint Strength slider. Good starting point.
- **Aggressive** (Strength = 1): No source injection at all — pure diffusion. Maximum creative freedom but less boundary consistency.

### Repaint Strength
Only active in **Balanced** mode. Drag to control the preserve-vs-regenerate tradeoff:
- **0** = Conservative behavior (auto-switches mode)
- **0.5** = Default — inject source during first 50% of steps
- **1** = Aggressive behavior (auto-switches mode)

### Tips
- Use "Send To Repaint" from Results to quickly load audio
- Start with Balanced (0.5) and adjust based on results
- Lower strength for better boundary consistency, higher for more creative output
help.generation_extract:
## Extract Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Stem separation — isolating instruments from a mix.

### Steps
1. Select **Extract** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Select **Track Name** to extract (e.g. vocals, drums, bass)
4. Click **Extract Stem**

### Available Tracks
vocals, backing_vocals, drums, bass, guitar, keyboard, percussion, strings, synth, fx, brass, woodwinds
help.generation_lego:
## Lego Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Adding new instrument tracks to existing audio.

### Steps
1. Select **Lego** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Select **Track Name** to add
4. Write a **Caption** describing the track
5. Click **Add Stem**

### Tips
- Great for layering: add drums to a guitar track, add bass to vocals
- The caption should describe only the new track's characteristics
help.generation_complete:
## Complete Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Auto-arranging — filling in missing instruments.

### Steps
1. Select **Complete** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio** (partial arrangement)
3. Select multiple **Track Names** to add
4. Write a **Caption** describing the desired style
5. Click **Generate Music**
help.generation_caption:
## Writing Good Captions

### Structure
A good caption includes:
- **Genre/Style**: pop, rock, jazz, electronic, classical…
- **Instruments**: guitar, piano, synth, drums, strings…
- **Mood**: upbeat, melancholic, energetic, dreamy…
- **Vocal style**: whispered, powerful, falsetto, rap…
- **Tempo feel**: fast, slow, moderate, driving…

### Examples
- *"Energetic pop-punk with distorted guitars, fast drums, and shouted vocals"*
- *"Smooth jazz trio with walking bass, brushed drums, and mellow piano"*
- *"Ambient electronic with layered synth pads and no vocals"*

### Tips
- More detail = better results
- Use the **Format** button to let AI enhance your caption
- Check 🎲 for example captions
help.generation_lyrics:
## Writing Lyrics

### Structure Tags
Use section tags to structure your song:
```
[Verse 1]
Your verse lyrics here

[Chorus]
Your chorus lyrics here

[Verse 2]
Second verse here

[Bridge]
Bridge section

[Outro]
Ending lyrics
```

### Tips
- Keep verses 4–8 lines
- Choruses should be memorable and repetitive
- Use `[Instrumental]` or `[Interlude]` for non-vocal sections
- Check **Instrumental** checkbox for pure instrumental music
- Select **Vocal Language** to match your lyrics language
help.generation_advanced:
## Advanced Settings

### Key Parameters
- **Inference Steps**: Turbo=8 (default), Base=up to 200. More steps ≠ always better for turbo
- **Guidance Scale**: Base model only. Higher = follows prompt more strictly
- **Shift**: Timestep shift (1.0–5.0). 3.0 recommended for turbo
- **Seed**: Set a specific seed for reproducible results

### LM Parameters
- **Temperature** (0.0–2.0): Higher = more creative/random
- **CFG Scale** (1.0–3.0): Higher = follows prompt more
- **Top-K / Top-P**: Sampling strategies for diversity

### Think Mode
Enable **Think** to use 5Hz LM for smarter generation:
- Generates semantic codes and metadata
- Requires LM to be initialized
- **ParallelThinking**: Process batches in parallel (faster)
help.results:
## Results Section

### Per-Sample Controls
- **Audio Player**: Play, pause, download
- **Send To Remix/Repaint**: Use this result as source for further editing
- **Save**: Export audio + metadata as JSON
- **Score**: Calculate quality score (perplexity-based)
- **LRC**: Generate lyrics timestamps

### Batch Navigation
- Use **◀ Previous** / **Next ▶** to browse batches
- Enable **AutoGen** to auto-generate next batch
- Click **Apply These Settings to UI** to reuse parameters from a good result

### Tips
- Generate 2–4 variations (batch size) and pick the best
- Use Score to objectively compare results
- Save good results for reference
help.training_dataset:
## Dataset Builder Tutorial

### Step 1: Load or Scan
- **Load**: Enter path to existing dataset JSON → Click Load
- **Scan**: Enter audio folder path → Click Scan
  - Supported: wav, mp3, flac, ogg, opus

### Step 2: Configure
- Set **Dataset Name**
- Check **All Instrumental** if no vocals
- Set **Custom Activation Tag** (unique trigger word for your LoRA)
- Choose **Tag Position**: Prepend, Append, or Replace

### Step 3: Auto-Label
- Click **Auto-Label All** to generate captions, BPM, key, time sig
- Use **Skip Metas** to skip BPM/Key/TimeSig (faster)

### Step 4: Preview & Edit
- Use slider to browse samples
- Edit caption, lyrics, BPM, key manually
- Click **Save Changes** per sample

### Step 5: Save
- Enter save path → Click **Save Dataset**

### Step 6: Preprocess
- Set tensor output directory → Click **Preprocess**
- This encodes audio/text to tensors for training

### 📖 Documentation
- [LoRA Training Tutorial](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/en/LoRA_Training_Tutorial.md) — Full step-by-step guide
- [Side-Step Advanced Training](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/sidestep/Getting%20Started.md) — CLI-based training with advanced features
help.training_train:
## LoRA Training Tutorial

### Setup
1. Enter **Preprocessed Tensors Directory** → Click **Load Dataset**
2. Configure LoRA:
   - **Rank** (r): 64 default. Higher = more capacity
   - **Alpha**: Usually 2× rank (128)
   - **Dropout**: 0.1 for regularization

### Training
3. Set **Learning Rate** (start with 1e-4)
4. Set **Max Epochs** (500 default)
5. Click **Start Training**
6. Monitor loss curve — it should decrease over time
7. Click **Stop Training** when satisfied

### Export
8. Enter export path → Click **Export LoRA**
9. Load in Settings: set LoRA Path → Load LoRA → Enable Use LoRA

### 🚀 Try LoKr for Faster Training
LoKr has greatly improved training efficiency. What used to take an hour now only takes 5 minutes — **over 10× faster**. This is crucial for training on consumer-grade GPUs. Switch to the **Train LoKr** tab to get started.

### Tips
- Use small batch size (1) if VRAM is limited
- Gradient accumulation increases effective batch size
- Save checkpoints frequently (every 200 epochs)

### 📖 Documentation
- [LoRA Training Tutorial](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/en/LoRA_Training_Tutorial.md) — Full step-by-step guide
- [Side-Step Advanced Training](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/sidestep/Getting%20Started.md) — CLI training with corrected timesteps, LoKR, VRAM optimization
help.training_lokr:
## 🚀 LoKr Training Tutorial

LoKr (Low-rank Kronecker product) has greatly improved training efficiency. What used to take an hour with LoRA now only takes 5 minutes — **over 10× faster**. This is crucial for training on consumer-grade GPUs.

### Setup
1. Enter **Preprocessed Tensors Directory** → Click **Load Dataset**
2. Configure LoKr:
   - **Linear Dim**: 64 default (similar to LoRA rank)
   - **Linear Alpha**: 128 default (scaling factor)
   - **Weight Decompose (DoRA)**: Enabled by default for better quality

### Training
3. Set **Learning Rate** (LoKr commonly uses higher LR, start with 1e-3)
4. Set **Max Epochs** (500 default)
5. Click **Start LoKr Training**
6. Monitor loss curve — it should decrease over time
7. Click **Stop Training** when satisfied

### Export
8. Enter export path → Click **Export LoKr**
9. Load in Settings: set LoRA Path → Load LoRA → Enable Use LoRA

### LoKr vs LoRA
| | LoKr | LoRA |
|---|---|---|
| Speed | ⚡ ~10× faster | Slower |
| VRAM | Lower | Higher |
| Quality | Comparable | Baseline |
| Best for | Consumer GPUs, rapid iteration | Maximum fidelity |

### Tips
- LoKr uses Kronecker decomposition for extreme efficiency
- Enable **DoRA** (Weight Decompose) for improved quality
- Use **Tucker decomposition** for additional compression
- Higher learning rate (1e-3) often works better than LoRA's typical 1e-4
help.training_export:
## Using Your Trained LoRA

### Export
1. After training, enter export path
2. Click **Export LoRA**

### Load & Use
1. In Settings, set **LoRA Path** to your exported directory
2. Click **Load LoRA**
3. Enable **Use LoRA** checkbox
4. Generate music — your LoRA style will be applied

### Tips
- Use your **Custom Activation Tag** in captions to trigger the style
- ⚠️ LoRA is incompatible with INT8 quantization
- You can unload and switch between different LoRAs

gen.enable_normalization_info: Normalize audio volume to target peak level to prevent clipping or ensure consistent loudness.
gen.normalization_db_info: Target peak level in decibels. -1.0 dB is standard safe peak. -0.1 dB is max.
gen.fade_in_duration_info: Apply a linear fade in to the beginning of the audio. 0 = no fade in. Range: 0–10 seconds.
gen.fade_out_duration_info: Apply a linear fade out to the end of the audio. 0 = no fade out. Range: 0–10 seconds.
gen.latent_shift_info: Shift applied to DiT latents before VAE decode. Default 0 (no shift). Negative values (e.g. -0.04) can reduce clipping.
gen.latent_rescale_info: Rescale factor for DiT latents before VAE decode. Default 1.0 (no rescale). Values < 1.0 (e.g. 0.91) can reduce clipping.
